title: Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae
intro: Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae, also known as the Andean mountain tea, is a plant species in the sunflower family, native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes in South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its use in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies, such as teas and infusions, to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, belonging to the genus Espeletiopsis. It is a member of the subfamily Mutisioideae, which comprises several genera of flowering plants native to the Americas. The exact classification of Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae may also be linked to its relationships within the larger Asteraceae family. Further study is required to confirm its classification and position within the plant kingdom. Espeletiopsis jimenez-quesadae is likely to be closely related to other members of the Mutisioideae subfamily.
